generated_comment: Sunna kya, Sharmaji ke bade bete ka? Usko toh us junior colleague ne poora phansa liya. Bechara Rahul, seedha-saadha ladka. Usko toh pata tha woh married hai, phir bhi. Aisi ghar todne wali auraton ki wajah se na, poori family badnaam hoti hai. Bilkul serial wali baat hai, na?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment demonstrates homewrecker framing by entirely blaming the 'other woman' for an affair ('usko poora phansa liya', 'ghar todne wali auraton') while portraying the man as a victim ('bechara Rahul'). It explicitly uses the 'she knew he was married' framing and includes a reference to Indian soap opera tropes.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rajan. Age 52. Married. A retired army officer in Pune. Your son just told you he wants to sign a prenup before his wedding next year. His fiance is upset about it. You are sitting with your son in the study and telling him whether you think he is right to want a prenup and what you think the fiance's reaction says about her intentions.
